channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: OpenAI pulls the plug on Sora, the viral AI video app that sparked deepfake concerns
item.description: OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.

(Image credit: Michael Dwyer)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Judge says government's Anthropic ban looks like punishment
item.description: Tech company Anthropic, the maker of the Claude AI system, is suing the Trump administration over the government labeling it a "supply chain risk."
item.content:encoded:
Tech company Anthropic, the maker of the Claude AI system, is suing the Trump administration over the government labeling it a "supply chain risk."

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: An air traffic controller was juggling extra roles during the LaGuardia plane crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board said it has concerns about air traffic controllers who work the midnight shift taking on extra work in an airspace as busy as LaGuardia's.
item.content:encoded:
The National Transportation Safety Board said it has concerns about air traffic controllers who work the midnight shift taking on extra work in an airspace as busy as LaGuardia's.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: New Mexico jury says Meta harms children's mental health and safety, violating state law
item.description: The jury agreed that Meta engaged in "unconscionable" trade practices that unfairly took advantage of the vulnerabilities of and inexperience of children. Jurors found there were thousands of violations, each counting separately toward a penalty of $375 million.
item.content:encoded:
The jury agreed that Meta engaged in "unconscionable" trade practices that unfairly took advantage of the vulnerabilities of and inexperience of children. Jurors found there were thousands of violations, each counting separately toward a penalty of $375 million.

(Image credit: Jim Weber/Santa Fe New Mexican)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Cuba sends doctors on medical missions. The U.S. isn't a fan
item.description: It's a major source of revenue for the island. And it's controversial. Now countries are sending Cuban doctors home in response to pressure from the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
It's a major source of revenue for the island. And it's controversial. Now countries are sending Cuban doctors home in response to pressure from the Trump administration.

(Image credit: Orlando Sierra/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Senate confirms Trump's pick for new role of fraud enforcement at Justice Department
item.description: The confirmation comes just days after the White House announced details of its own task force to pursue fraud in government programs.
item.content:encoded:
The confirmation comes just days after the White House announced details of its own task force to pursue fraud in government programs.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: The Israeli military wants several more weeks to fight Iran war, officials say
item.description: The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.
item.content:encoded:
The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Her mother murdered her father in an infamous case. Now, she's telling her own story
item.description: Lucille Miller was convicted of killing her husband in 1965. Now her daughter Debra reflects on her own traumatic childhood and its lingering effects in The Most Wonderful Terrible Person.
item.content:encoded: Lucille Miller was convicted of killing her husband in 1965. Now her daughter Debra reflects on her own traumatic childhood and its lingering effects in The Most Wonderful Terrible Person.
item.dc:creator: Ilana Masad

item.title: Congress loses a flying perk as DHS shutdown continues
item.description: Delta Airlines is temporarily suspending specialty services to member of Congress due to resource constraints from the ongoing shutdown of DHS.
item.content:encoded:
Delta Airlines is temporarily suspending specialty services to member of Congress due to resource constraints from the ongoing shutdown of DHS.

(Image credit: SAUL LOEB/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt
